significant_events.event_name: AWBI Adopts ABC-AR as National Policy
significant_events.source_quote: Animal Welfare Board Of India (AWBI) adopts ABC-AR making it a national policy in India.
significant_events.summary: The Animal Welfare Board of India (AWBI) adopted the Animal Birth Control-Anti-Rabies (ABC-AR) program, making it a national policy in India.

significant_events.event_name: Government of India Notifies 'Animal Birth Control (Dogs) Rules'
significant_events.source_quote: The Government Of India notifies 'Animal Birth Control (Dogs) Rules'. Making ABC-AR a legal reqirement throughout India.
significant_events.summary: The Government of India made ABC-AR a legal requirement throughout India by notifying the 'Animal Birth Control (Dogs) Rules'.

significant_events.event_name: Successful Campaign to End Export of Frog Legs and Use of Animal Cadavers
significant_events.source_quote: The Blue Cross also campaigned successfully to end the export of frog legs to Europe and the use of animal cadavers in biology dissection.
significant_events.summary: Blue Cross of India successfully campaigned to end the export of frog legs to Europe and the use of animal cadavers in biology dissection.

significant_events.event_name: Mass Vaccination of Dogs at Kannagi Nagar
significant_events.source_quote: It all started on 12th March 2023, and on two subsequent days, at Kannagai Nagar, Thoraipakkam, Chennai, where over 1000 community dogs underwent vaccination to protect them deadly viral diseases like CD, Parvo....
significant_events.summary: Over 1000 community dogs underwent vaccination to protect them from deadly viral diseases like CD and Parvo, sponsored by HCLFoundation.

significant_events.source_quote: Two more Mobile Hospital (MoHo) vehicles, sponsored by DahNay Logistics and Ranka Steels were inaugurated by Mr. Murali Babu of DahNay Logistics and Mr.Mahesh Kumar, Deputy Mayor of Greater Chennai Corporation in November 22 and March 23 respectively and is being used for providing on-site treatment to the distressed animals.
significant_events.summary: Two new Mobile Hospital (MoHo) vehicles, sponsored by DahNay Logistics and Ranka Steels, were inaugurated for on-site treatment of distressed animals.

significant_events.source_quote: Blue Cross of India's annual project of distributing water bowls was done this year also. Free water bowl distribution has engaged a lot of volunteer participation and helped thousands of animals quench their thirst.
significant_events.summary: Annual project distributing water bowls to help thousands of animals and birds quench their thirst, with significant volunteer participation.

significant_events.source_quote: Blue Cross of India observed World Rabies Day by vaccinating all the permanent resident dogs in BCI and also conducted an Anti Rabies vaccination drive in Pallikaranai area by vaccinating around 150 dogs.
significant_events.summary: Observed World Rabies Day by vaccinating all permanent resident dogs in BCI and around 150 dogs in Pallikaranai area.

significant_events.source_quote: The Great Indian Dog Show at Phoenix Mall on 24th April 2022
significant_events.summary: Special adoptathon event held at Phoenix Mall on April 24th, 2022.

significant_events.source_quote: She inaugurated and dedicated the HCLFoundation sponsored Lab for the animals' welfare.
significant_events.summary: HCLFoundation sponsored a new lab for animal welfare, inaugurated by Ms. Nidhi Pundhir in December 2022.

significant_events.source_quote: The modular kennels, 13 in number, sponsored by Mr.Ravindar and Mrs.Malliga Ravindar were inaugurated in January 2023
significant_events.summary: 13 modular kennels, sponsored by Mr. Ravindar and Mrs. Malliga Ravindar, were inaugurated in January 2023.

significant_events.event_name: Stopping Street Animal Culling in Madras (1996)
significant_events.source_quote: Blue Cross Of India is instrumental in stopping street animal culling in 1996 in the city of Madras. Other cities in India soon follow.
significant_events.summary: Blue Cross Of India was instrumental in stopping street animal culling in 1996 in the city of Madras, with other cities in India soon following.

significant_events.source_quote: In 2009, the city was declared rabies-free, a testament to the organization's tireless work.
significant_events.summary: Chennai was declared rabies-free in 2009, attributed to the organization's tireless work, particularly aggressive ABC-AR programs.

data.original_text: Blue Cross of India (BCI) runs hospitals and shelters for injured or unwell stray dogs, cats, cattle, horses, and birds in Chennai.
They also provide animal ambulances and a mobile dispensary.
The non-medical division of the organisation is looked after by volunteers.
It runs several animal welfare programs including animal rights awareness.
Activities   Medical waste disposal.   Shelters.   Rehoming.   Adoption.   ABC - Animal Birth Control.   Hospitals.   Mobile Dispensary.   Work with Other NGOs.   Ambulance Services.
BCI began to rescue, spay and vaccinate street dogs and also persuaded pet owners and people taking care of street dogs to bring them for treatment free of cost.
Blue Cross of India is home to dogs, cattle and birds and many more creatures.
They are orphaned, abandoned, old, injured, abused and physically disabled by speeding vehicles and cruel minds.
The volume of our task in 2015: About 5637 animals rescued.
Over 1400 animals are present in our Guindy shelter at any point of time and over 100 cattle and 50 dogs in our Kunnam Shelter and a further 45 dogs at our Toducadu shelter.
15604 Dogs and 402 cats spayed and vaccinated.
9717 out-patient handled. 23243 in-patient handed.
The organization has received several national and international awards.
On July 27, 1994, thirty years after the organisation's founding, it received a gift of 4 acres (16,000 m2) of land and a donation of ₹ 25,00,000.
This land was purchased a few months later so that they could have a permanent shelter.

Dr. S. Chinny Krishna has been awarded the Jeev Daya Puraskar by the Government of India, the Venu Menon Life Time achievement award, the Elizabeth Lleyt Award of the North Shore Animal League, The Middle East Network for Animal Welfare's Lifetime Achievement Award, The Jeanne Marching Award, and many others including the Kanuna Award.

The Blue Cross has received the Proggy Award from PETA for its most innovative and interactive computer programmes which serve as an alternate to dissection of animals, the Bhagavan Mahaveer Ahimsa Foundation Award; and the Hastimull Award.
Initially started as a small group, it grew to become one of the country's largest animal welfare organizations, running active animal welfare, animal rights and humane education programmes.

The Blue Cross of India engages in a wide range of activities aimed at improving animal welfare. These include: Animal Birth Control (ABC) Program: The organization has been instrumental in implementing the ABC-AR (Animal Birth Control-Anti-Rabies) program, which has been highly effective in managing street animal populations and preventing rabies.
Rehoming and Adoption: The organization works tirelessly to find new homes for animals that are orphaned, abandoned, or injured.
Blue Cross Of India is instrumental in stopping street animal culling in 1996 in the city of Madras. Other cities in India soon follow.
Animal Welfare Board Of India (AWBI) adopts ABC-AR making it a national policy in India.
ABC-AR is cited as one of the watershed moments in world environmental history and has been called out by the Radford University on their Environmental History Timeline.
The Government Of India notifies 'Animal Birth Control (Dogs) Rules'. Making ABC-AR a legal reqirement throughout India.
T​HE FIRST IN THE WORLD TO START ANIMAL BIRTH CONTROL SURGERIES TO MANAGE STREET DOG POPULATION & PREVENT RABIES.
Blue Cross Of India was the first AWO in India to receive the Guidestar Platinum Rating in 2013-14 and continues to hold this rating.
Jaipur in 2003 and Madras in 2007 record 'Zero' human deaths due to rabies​ for the first time since records were kept, primarily attributed to the agressive ABC-AR programmes in these cities.

Following this radical change in approach to dog management, the number of human rabies cases fell sharply from 120 cases the year before the ABC program was launched to zero human cases within a decade.
Still, Chennai is again back to zero (or very close to zero) human rabies cases.
The Blue Cross also campaigned successfully to end the export of frog legs to Europe and the use of animal cadavers in biology dissection.
In 2009, the city was declared rabies-free, a testament to the organization's tireless work.
The organization also conducts humane education programs to promote animal welfare and animal rights.
Additionally, BCI operates shelters and facilitates adoptions, offering in-patient and out-patient veterinary services, including ambulances and facilities for animal cremations.
Through its collaborative approach, BCI works closely with other NGOs in animal welfare, sharing resources and expertise to maximize their impact.

The Blue Cross of India is a charitable animal welfare society founded in 1959 in Chennai by Captain V. Sundaram, his wife Usha Sundaram, and their son S. Chinny Krishna, dedicated to alleviating the suffering of stray and abandoned animals through shelters, veterinary hospitals, sterilization surgeries, rabies vaccinations, and adoption programs.

Its mission emphasizes empirical interventions like ABC combined with anti-rabies vaccinations, which have demonstrably reduced rabies incidence and human fatalities in Chennai by addressing root causes of overpopulation and disease transmission among street dogs.

Key achievements include successful advocacy for national bans on exporting frogs' legs and turtle shells in the 1980s, alongside operating facilities that treat cattle, cats, birds, and other species, fostering adoptions and rehabilitation without reliance on euthanasia as a primary method.
17,454 calls were responded to and 5,637 animals were rescued. This includes 3511 dogs, 1322 puppies, 219 cats, 333 kittens, 122 birds, 106 cattle, 21 squirrels, 2 goats, and 1 piglet.
45 cattle rescued from illegal transport during the year are currently with us and cases have been filed by us.
In addition to the above, 402 cats were spayed, vaccinated and returned to their localities.
They also conducted ABC procedures for 284 dogs and 16 cats.
